locked_facts.potential_gradient_formula: k = V / L where V is driver cell voltage and L is wire length
locked_facts.balance_condition: EMF of cell equals potential difference across balancing length: E = k * l
locked_facts.comparison_of_EMFs: E1/E2 = l1/l2 where l1 and l2 are balancing lengths
locked_facts.internal_resistance_formula: r = R * (l1 - l2) / l2 where R is external resistance

ncert_truth_statements: A potentiometer works on the principle that the potential difference across any portion of a uniform wire is proportional to its length when a steady current flows through it
At the balance point, the galvanometer reads zero because the EMF of the cell being tested exactly equals the potential difference across the balancing length: E = kl
The potentiometer measures the true EMF of a cell because it draws no current from the cell at balance, unlike a voltmeter which draws a small current
The ratio of two EMFs can be compared using a potentiometer: E₁/E₂ = l₁/l₂, where l₁ and l₂ are the respective balancing lengths
The internal resistance of a cell can be measured with a potentiometer using the formula r = R(l₁ − l₂)/l₂, where R is the external resistance and l₁, l₂ are balancing lengths with and without R
